title: U-Shaped School Desk
summary: A six-foot U-shaped desk with two return wings — built for homeschool corners and shared workspaces, from 1x10 tops on a 2x4 frame with built-up legs.
description:
A U-shaped desk is the most workspace you can fit against one wall: a six-foot main run with two 24" returns, so two kids work side by side or one spreads out across three surfaces. This build gets there with humble stock — 1x10 tops, a 1x3-and-2x4 frame, and six legs each built up from a 2x4 laminated to a 2x2, which gives an L-profile leg that looks more furniture than framing.

The frame goes together first as a U (two return frames, back apron, inner supports), then the legs attach with — the plan's own emphasis — a LEVEL, not a tape measure, checking each leg as it's screwed. On a piece with six legs, level-as-you-go is the only method that doesn't end in shimming.

The top is where you choose your finish quality: screw the 1x10s down individually (fast, rustic, visible seams), or take the plan's tip and pocket-hole the whole top into one panel first, then attach it as a unit — flatter, cleaner, and worth it on a desk where pencils will find every seam. Either way, finish with polyurethane; a school desk's topcoat gets erased, markered, and glue-sticked, and poly is what shrugs that off.

tools.note: The plan says predrill the 2" screws for strength — 1x3 near its ends splits without pilots.
Tacking the built-up legs while glue sets.
The legs are attached 'using a level' — it beats the tape on a six-legged piece.
The nicer-top option: join the 1x10s into one panel first.
120 grit over every surface — forearms live on this desk.

materials.name: Self-tapping wood screws, #8 x 2", star drive
Polyurethane
materials.note: The desk top's armor. Recommended by the plan, seconded by every parent.

steps.title: Build the two return frames
steps.body: Assemble the two side frames from 1x3s per the diagram — predrill and use the 2" screws; 1x3 stock splits near its ends without pilot holes.
materials: Self-tapping wood screws, #8 x 2", star drive

steps.title: Connect with the back apron
steps.body: Screw the 68-1/2" back apron across the two return frames. The U now exists in outline.
materials: Self-tapping wood screws, #8 x 2", star drive

steps.title: Add the inner support frame
steps.body: Attach the inner front rail per the diagram — it stiffens the main span and gives the desktop its front landing.
materials: Self-tapping wood screws, #8 x 2", star drive

steps.title: Build six L-profile legs
steps.body: Glue and brad each 2x2 to a 2x4, edges flush, making six L-section legs. The L is stiffer than either board alone and reads as a deliberate furniture leg instead of framing.

steps.title: Attach the legs — with a level in hand
steps.body: Screw each leg to its frame corner with multiple 2" screws and glue, checking the leg plumb with a level AS you drive. Six legs on three frames: the level-as-you-go method is the only one that ends with all six touching the floor.
materials: Self-tapping wood screws, #8 x 2", star drive

steps.title: Add the blocking
steps.body: Screw the 2x4 blocking pieces in per the diagram — they back up the desktop seams where the returns meet the main run.
materials: Self-tapping wood screws, #8 x 2", star drive

steps.title: Lay the main desktop
steps.body:
Position the two 72" 1x10s and screw down into the frame.

The better-top option, straight from the plan: pocket-hole ALL the top boards into one flat panel first, then attach the finished panel. Extra hour, visibly nicer desk — take the option if the desk lives anywhere guests see.
materials: Self-tapping wood screws, #8 x 2", star drive

steps.title: Finish the returns and armor the top
steps.body: Add the four return desktop pieces and screw down. Fill every hole and seam, sand to 120, prime and paint (or stain), and topcoat the entire work surface with polyurethane — two coats minimum. School desks are finished for war, not for looks.
materials: Self-tapping wood screws, #8 x 2", star drive

images.alt: U-shaped wooden desk with six-foot main surface and two return wings
